verses.text: Slandered and surrounded by lying, hateful words, David prays, 'Hold not thy peace, O God of my praise.' He appeals to God against false tongues. When wronged by deceit, the believer turns to the God who knows the truth.

Slandered and surrounded by lying, hateful words, David prays, 'Hold not thy peace, O God of my praise.' He appeals to God against false tongues. When wronged by deceit, the believer turns to the God who knows the truth.

Slandered and surrounded by lying, hateful words, David prays, 'Hold not thy peace, O God of my praise.' He appeals to God against false tongues. When wronged by deceit, the believer turns to the God who knows the truth.
For his love they are his adversaries, repaying 'evil for good, and hatred for my love.' Ingratitude wounds him. To be hated for loving is among the bitterest of trials, and one Christ bore fully.
For his love they are his adversaries, repaying 'evil for good, and hatred for my love.' Ingratitude wounds him. To be hated for loving is among the bitterest of trials, and one Christ bore fully.
He calls for judgment on the chief offender, that his office be given to another. Peter applies this to Judas. The betrayer of innocence brings a righteous curse upon himself.

He prays that the wicked man's gains be seized and his line cut off, his sins ever before God. The plea is severe. Such psalms commit unrepented cruelty to the just judgment of God.

Because the man loved cursing and never showed mercy, persecuting the poor, 'so let it come unto him.' His own malice returns on him. The mercilessness we sow we may be left to reap.

'But do thou for me, O God the Lord, for thy name's sake,' he prays, poor, needy, and wasted away. He turns from his foes to God. The afflicted soul rests its case on God's mercy and honour.

He asks God to help and save, that men may know it is His hand, while his accusers are clothed with shame. He longs for God to be seen. Deliverance answers the slanderer and glorifies God.

He asks God to help and save, that men may know it is His hand, while his accusers are clothed with shame. He longs for God to be seen. Deliverance answers the slanderer and glorifies God.
He will greatly praise the LORD, who 'shall stand at the right hand of the poor' to save him. Confidence ends the psalm. God takes His place beside the needy as their defender.
